summary: Completed full backend + frontend e2e testing for Shelfsort EPUB sorter. 17/17 pytest backend tests pass; UI flows all verified (landing, login Google button, /library auth gate, upload, book card with HP fandom badge, book detail with all 4 action buttons, fandom chips). AI reclassify via Emergent LLM key works.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~670 lines and includes auth, EPUB parsing, classification, and routes in one file — consider splitting into modules (auth.py, epub.py, classify.py, routes_books.py) as it grows.
GET /api/books/export/zip builds the entire ZIP in memory before streaming — for large libraries this can spike memory; consider using a true streaming zip writer or tempfile.

auth_google trusts data['session_token'] from upstream and stores it as the session token; this is by design for Emergent OAuth but worth documenting; expires_at uses our own 7-day TTL rather than upstream-provided expiry.
cover endpoint returns media_type='image/jpeg' for all covers regardless of actual image format (could be PNG/WebP); minor mismatch but works in browsers.
FANDOM_KEYWORDS scan is O(N*K) on lowercased blob — fine at current size but could be precompiled to a regex aho-corasick if catalog grows.
test_credentials: Emergent Google OAuth only. For tests, seeded user + session in MongoDB (db: test_database, collections: users + user_sessions) and authenticated via Authorization: Bearer <session_token> or session_token cookie.
seed_data_creation: Tests create per-run user/session and clean up via fixture teardown. UI test seeded user_ui_c14c35ca with session token ui_sess_247bfd71...; safe to leave or purge.
context_for_next_testing_agent: backend_test.py is reusable pytest suite with 17 tests covering health, auth, upload+classification (HP/Twilight/Non-fiction), list/filter, stats, get/cover/download, patch, reclassify (real Claude via EMERGENT_LLM_KEY), export zip, categories CRUD, delete. To run: pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v. UI: seed cookie session_token and navigate to /library; set_input_files on input[type=file] for upload.
